title: interview intent
description: Structured campaign intent produced by an interview between the hpc agent and either MARs or a human. Deliberately bare-bones: the schema captures *why* a campaign was created (goal, transcript, provenance), *how big* it is (task_count), *what tags* identify it for future recall (task_kind), and *what limits* apply (budget, abort_if). It does NOT typed-encode the search space — that would narrow the existing experiment-agnostic tasks.py contract (total() + resolve(i) -> dict[str, Any]; the dict shape is enforced by compute_cmd_sha because kwargs get **-unpacked into the user's task function). The interview agent is responsible for writing the tasks.py itself; this primitive validates that the produced tasks.py matches the recorded intent (task_count cross-check, cmd_sha fingerprint) and persists the intent alongside it. The reserved `task_generator` field is a placeholder for an optional future opt-in: when present, a typed materializer regenerates tasks.py from the recipe; when absent, the interview agent's hand-written tasks.py is the source of truth.

goal.description: Free-text campaign goal, ~one sentence. Captures intent that structured fields can't ('find LR for vit-b on imagenet-1k @ 8 GPUs', 'eval opus-4.7 vs sonnet-4.6 on MMLU-pro', 'rollout PPO on cartpole over 10 seeds').

task_count.description: Expected number of tasks. The materializer asserts tasks.total() == task_count and refuses to write interview.json on mismatch — catches off-by-one bugs in the agent-written tasks.py at the interview stage.

task_kind.description: Free-text tag identifying the campaign family for recall queries ('ml-hparam-sweep', 'rl-rollout', 'llm-prompt-eval', 'benchmark-perf', 'data-shard'). No enum — new families are encouraged. cmd_recall groups by this tag.

budget.description: Soft caps. Opaque dict — the interview agent picks the units that match the campaign (gpu_hours for ML, cpu_hours for HPC, task_count for cheap evals, credits for hosted-model spend). campaign-flow surfaces these in its progress envelope; it does not enforce them by killing jobs.

abort_if.description: Optional early-stop criterion. Loose shape — the only required field is `metric` (the key the aggregator emits) and `after_tasks` (how many tasks must complete before the check is meaningful). campaign-flow consumes this; interview just records it.

cluster_target.description: Optional. When present, submit_flow uses these directly and meta.json is updated to match. When omitted, the planner is invoked to choose.

produced_by.description: Who/what produced this intent. Indexed by cmd_recall so future interviews can surface 'last time you ran this kind of campaign…' summaries.

transcript.description: Interview Q/A turns. Optional but strongly recommended for human interviews — the value of being able to explain 'why did this campaign target cluster X with this LR range' three months later vastly outweighs the few KB of storage. For MARs interviews this is typically the agent's tool-call trace.

notes.description: Free-form supplementary notes from the interview (caveats, deferred decisions, things the operator said 'maybe' about). Distinct from `transcript` in that notes are the agent's summary, not the literal turns.

task_generator.description: Optional opt-in: when present, the materializer regenerates tasks.py from a typed recipe instead of consuming the agent-written one. The five shapes below cover the common ground without locking out exotic campaigns — anything that doesn't fit drops `task_generator` from intent and the interview agent writes tasks.py by hand. The materializer cross-checks the produced task count against `intent.task_count` before any disk writes, so an off-by-one in the recipe surfaces as spec_invalid rather than as a clobbered tasks.py.

items.description: List of task-kwargs dicts. Most agnostic shape — just stores the list verbatim and resolve(i) returns items[i].

axes.description: Named axes; tasks = full Cartesian product. resolve(i) returns a dict with one key per axis.

params.description: Cross every item with every seed. resolve(i) returns {**items[i // len(seeds)], 'seed': seeds[i % len(seeds)]}. The 'seed' key in items is overwritten.

session_sha.description: MARs session identifier. Required when kind=mars; null otherwise.

operator.description: Human operator name/handle when kind=human; null for MARs.
